rows.version_or_status: AICPA SOC 1 service organization examination focused on controls relevant to user entities' internal control over financial reporting.
missing_evidence: Define whether this service affects customer financial reporting controls.
Create auditor-scoped system description and control objectives.
Establish control period, management assertion, sampling method, and external auditor engagement.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no SOC 1 attestation or ICFR assurance claim.
next_actions: Have finance control owner and external auditor decide whether SOC 1 is in scope.
Map ledger, reconciliation, idempotency, access, change, and incident controls to auditor-selected control objectives.

rows.version_or_status: AICPA SOC 2 service organization examination over security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, or privacy categories selected for scope.
missing_evidence: Define SOC 2 system boundary, trust services categories, control period, owners, and evidence cadence.
Add production identity, access review, vulnerability management, vendor risk, incident response, backup, and change management evidence.
Engage qualified auditor for readiness review and examination.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no SOC 2 attestation or trust services assurance claim.
next_actions: Use Security, Availability, Confidentiality, and Processing Integrity as default candidate categories; keep Privacy conditional on personal data scope.
Build a control owner matrix and evidence calendar before any external auditor review.

rows.version_or_status: General-use SOC report path for controls relevant to selected trust services categories.
missing_evidence: Complete SOC 2-style control scope first.
Obtain auditor determination that a general-use report is appropriate.
Prepare public distribution review and legal review process.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no SOC 3 report or public assurance claim.
next_actions: Defer until SOC 2 scope and external auditor path are selected.

rows.version_or_status: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 information security management system requirements.
missing_evidence: Define ISMS scope, risk assessment method, statement of applicability, control owners, internal audit cadence, and management review cadence.
Add policies for asset management, human resources, supplier risk, incident management, vulnerability management, access reviews, and business continuity.
Engage accredited certification body if external certification is pursued.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no ISO/IEC 27001 certification or ISMS conformance claim.
next_actions: Create ISMS scope and risk register outside this demo repo.
Map repo evidence to ISO 27001 Annex A controls after a control owner chooses the scope.

rows.version_or_status: ISO/IEC 27017:2015 cloud security guidance confirmed current in 2024; replacement version under publication as of research date.
missing_evidence: Define cloud service provider versus cloud service customer role.
Map shared responsibility, tenant isolation, IAM, logging, encryption, and customer responsibilities to selected ISO 27017 controls.
Update the row when ISO publishes the replacement edition.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no ISO/IEC 27017 certification, extension, or cloud-control assurance claim.
next_actions: Monitor the ISO 27017 replacement publication and update version metadata.
Tie AWS shared responsibility and ECS task-role evidence to the chosen cloud-control scope.

rows.version_or_status: ISO/IEC 27018:2025 guidance for protection of PII in public clouds acting as PII processors.
missing_evidence: Determine whether production scope processes PII in a public cloud processor role.
Add data inventory, processing instructions, deletion workflow, subprocessors, breach workflow, and privacy contract evidence.
Engage privacy owner and auditor if PII cloud assurance is pursued.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no ISO/IEC 27018 certification, PII processor, or privacy assurance claim.
next_actions: Keep conditional until production data inventory confirms PII processing.

rows.version_or_status: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 privacy information management system requirements and guidance for PII controllers and processors.
missing_evidence: Define PII controller and processor roles.
Add privacy governance, lawful basis, data subject request, retention, transfer, DPIA, and incident notification workflows.
Select certification body requirements if PIMS certification is pursued.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no ISO/IEC 27701 certification or PIMS claim.
next_actions: Require privacy counsel and privacy owner scope decision before treating this row as applicable.

rows.version_or_status: ISO 22301:2019 business continuity management system requirements with 2024 amendment.
missing_evidence: Define business impact analysis, recovery objectives, crisis roles, continuity plan, exercise calendar, and supplier continuity evidence.
Add production backup/restore and disaster recovery runbooks.
Run severe-but-plausible scenario exercises with owner signoff.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no ISO 22301 certification or BCMS claim.
next_actions: Map existing FIS and smoke evidence to future continuity exercises after production services are defined.

rows.version_or_status: ISO/IEC 20000-1:2018 service management system requirements with 2024 amendment.
missing_evidence: Define live service catalog, service-level objectives, incident, problem, change, release, configuration, supplier, and continual improvement processes.
Add production support ownership and service reporting evidence.
Engage service management owner if external certification is pursued.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no ISO/IEC 20000-1 certification or service-management-system claim.
next_actions: Keep as future external readiness until a production service management scope exists.

rows.version_or_status: PCI DSS v4.0.1 current PCI SSC document family for cardholder data environments.
missing_evidence: Current repo has no cardholder data environment, payment account data flow, merchant role, acquirer requirement, or card processing integration.
If cardholder data enters scope, create a PCI scope inventory, network segmentation model, QSA/SAQ path, and evidence plan.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no PCI DSS validation or cardholder-data-environment claim.
next_actions: Keep not applicable unless product scope changes to process, store, or transmit payment account data.

rows.version_or_status: CSA Cloud Controls Matrix and CAIQ v4.1 released 2026-01-27 with 207 controls across 17 domains.
missing_evidence: Create a CAIQ answer set and evidence references if customer cloud assurance questionnaires become in scope.
Map repo controls to CCM domains after production cloud service boundary is defined.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no CSA CCM assessment or CAIQ submission claim.
next_actions: Use CCM domains as a crosswalk for cloud control gaps.

rows.version_or_status: CSA Security, Trust, Assurance, and Risk program and registry for cloud security assurance.
missing_evidence: Decide whether this project is a cloud provider offering that should publish STAR evidence.
Prepare CAIQ/CCM evidence and select Level 1 self-assessment or Level 2 third-party path if applicable.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no CSA STAR registry, self-assessment, or third-party audit claim.
next_actions: Defer until a customer-facing cloud service and assurance strategy exist.

rows.version_or_status: FTC Safeguards Rule for financial institutions under FTC jurisdiction handling customer information.
missing_evidence: Determine whether the operator is a financial institution under FTC jurisdiction.
Define customer information classes, written security program, qualified individual, risk assessment, safeguards, monitoring, service provider oversight, incident response, and reporting workflow.
Add production evidence for access control, encryption, vulnerability management, logging, training, and board/senior officer reporting.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no GLBA or FTC Safeguards Rule legal conclusion.
next_actions: Route applicability to counsel and compliance owner before assigning mandatory controls.

rows.version_or_status: SEC 2024 Regulation S-P amendments include customer information safeguarding, transfer agent scope expansion, written records, and incident response requirements.
missing_evidence: Determine whether the operator is a covered institution or registered transfer agent.
Define sensitive customer information, incident response program, notification workflow, service provider oversight, disposal workflow, and written records.
Add production data inventory and customer notification evidence if in scope.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no SEC Regulation S-P applicability or regulatory conclusion.
next_actions: Route to securities counsel because transfer-agent references in the demo domain are not evidence of actual registered transfer-agent status.

rows.version_or_status: SEC systems compliance and integrity rules for SCI entities such as certain SROs, registered clearing agencies, ATSs, plan processors, and exempt clearing agencies.
missing_evidence: Determine whether the operator is an SCI entity.
If in scope, define SCI systems, indirect SCI systems, policies, review cadence, testing, notification, recordkeeping, and regulatory reporting evidence.
Add production resilience and market-participant communication processes.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no SEC Regulation SCI applicability or SCI-entity claim.
next_actions: Keep conditional unless counsel confirms SCI entity status.

rows.version_or_status: FINRA cybersecurity checklist derived primarily from NIST CSF and FINRA cybersecurity practices.
missing_evidence: Determine whether the operator is a FINRA member or broker-dealer affiliate.
Add production cybersecurity program, inventory, access, vendor, detection, response, training, and recovery evidence if in scope.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no FINRA member, broker-dealer, or regulatory conclusion.
next_actions: Use the checklist as a small-firm cybersecurity crosswalk only after membership scope is confirmed.

rows.version_or_status: NYDFS cybersecurity regulation, 23 NYCRR Part 500, amended November 1, 2023 with phased requirements.
missing_evidence: Determine whether the operator is a covered entity or qualifies for an exemption.

Add cybersecurity program, CISO governance, MFA, asset inventory, vulnerability management, audit trail, incident response, business continuity, third-party service provider, and annual filing evidence if in scope.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no NYDFS Part 500 applicability or filing claim.
next_actions: Route to NY financial-services counsel and security owner if a NYDFS-covered entity is involved.

rows.framework: GDPR and UK data protection readiness
rows.version_or_status: EU GDPR legal text and European Commission data protection guidance; UK scope requires separate UK data protection review.
missing_evidence: Determine whether EU or UK personal data is processed.
Add lawful basis, records of processing, data subject rights, retention, deletion, DPIA, international transfers, subprocessors, breach workflow, and privacy notice evidence.
Determine controller, processor, joint-controller, and representative roles.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no GDPR, UK GDPR, or privacy-law conclusion.
next_actions: Keep conditional until production data inventory and privacy counsel review exist.

rows.version_or_status: Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 on digital operational resilience for the financial sector, applied since 2025-01-17.
missing_evidence: Determine whether the operator is an EU financial entity or ICT third-party service provider in DORA scope.
Add ICT risk management, incident classification/reporting, resilience testing, third-party risk management, exit strategy, and register of information evidence if in scope.
Define production impact tolerances and severe-but-plausible test calendar.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no DORA applicability or digital-operational-resilience legal conclusion.
next_actions: Use current FIS and liveness evidence as a seed for future resilience testing after scope is confirmed.

rows.version_or_status: UK operational resilience expectations for important business services, impact tolerances, testing, and disruption reporting.
missing_evidence: Determine whether a UK regulated firm, PRA-designated firm, or critical third-party context exists.
Define important business services, impact tolerances, mapping, testing plan, communications, lessons learned, and self-assessment evidence.
Add production service owner and risk owner approvals before external use.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no FCA, PRA, or UK operational-resilience regulatory conclusion.
next_actions: Map smoke, queue-drain, and FIS evidence to future severe-but-plausible scenario testing after scope is known.

rows.version_or_status: NIST CSF 2.0, February 26, 2024, with Govern, Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, and Recover functions.
missing_evidence: Create explicit CSF profile with target outcomes, current outcomes, owners, and maturity scoring.
Add production Detect, Respond, and Recover operational evidence.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no NIST CSF external assurance or maturity claim.
next_actions: Use CSF as the top-level governance taxonomy for future cross-framework mapping.

rows.version_or_status: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5, update 1 page, with control catalog for systems and organizations.
missing_evidence: Select control baseline, tailoring assumptions, overlays, assessment procedures, and organization-defined parameters.
Add production access control, audit, configuration, contingency, risk, system communications, and system integrity evidence.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no NIST authorization, assessment, or federal system claim.
next_actions: Map current evidence to selected control families after a target baseline is chosen.

rows.version_or_status: CIS Controls v8.1 prioritized safeguards with updated CSF 2.0 Govern alignment.
missing_evidence: Select implementation group and map asset inventory, account inventory, vulnerability management, audit log management, malware defense, data recovery, and security awareness controls.
Add production endpoint, cloud account, vulnerability scanner, and SIEM evidence.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no CIS assessment or implementation-group maturity claim.
next_actions: Use CIS as a practical engineering checklist after production asset inventory exists.

rows.framework: AWS Well-Architected and Financial Services Industry Lens
rows.version_or_status: AWS Well-Architected Framework and AWS Financial Services Industry Lens for cloud workload design and review.
missing_evidence: Run a formal AWS Well-Architected Tool review for any future production workload.
Add production account structure, backup, monitoring, incident, access, cost, and resilience evidence.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no AWS Well-Architected review completion or FSI regulatory alignment claim.
next_actions: Use the completed AWS sandbox report as engineering evidence, not production cloud assurance.

rows.framework: AWS shared responsibility, IAM, ECS, FIS, SNS/SQS, KMS, CloudWatch, and Artifact
rows.version_or_status: AWS shared responsibility and service-specific guidance for cloud security and evidence collection.
missing_evidence: For production, collect AWS Artifact reports in an access-controlled evidence room outside the repo.
Add production IAM Access Analyzer review, CloudTrail, GuardDuty, Security Hub, Config, backup, and account baseline evidence.
Define customer-versus-AWS responsibility matrix for each managed service in production.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; no AWS provider-report inheritance or production cloud-control assurance claim.
next_actions: Keep AWS Artifact downloads out of this repo and reference them by evidence-room identifier if used.

rows.version_or_status: Internal production-readiness boundary informed by AWS Well-Architected, ISO 22301, ISO/IEC 20000-1, NIST CSF, and repo rules.
missing_evidence: Add production identity, authorization, TLS, secrets, data classification, data retention, backup, disaster recovery, monitoring, alerting, incident response, change approval, vendor risk, and rollout controls.
Define production account, network, data, operator, support, and escalation ownership.
Create production ADRs and deployment gates before any production use.
rows.claim_boundary: Internal readiness only; not production ready and no production deployment authorization.
next_actions: Keep local and AWS sandbox evidence separate from production authorization.
Open a separate production control-plane workstream if production adaptation is requested.
